steps.title: Trace the causal claim
steps.content: The passage begins by describing people as creatures of sun, light, and life. It then gives the cause: these traits explain why people crowd into towns and cities while the country grows deserted. The night description develops the same idea, showing that darkness and rural loneliness make people long for streets and human voices.

steps.title: Reject narrower readings
steps.content: Choice B places isolation in crowded areas, but the loneliness occurs in the dark countryside. Choice C reverses the preference by emphasizing hillsides over cities. Choice D gives advice about valuing rural beauty, while the passage explains a motive for urban living. The answer is A.
